name: Saeed ibn Abdullah
title: Shield of the Prayer
role: Loyal Companion and Martyr of Prayer
shortDescription: A prominent Shia figure in Kufa who made himself a shield against enemy arrows on Ashura noon so the Imam could finish his prayer.
fullStory:
Saeed ibn Abdullah al-Hanafi was a prominent and brave Shia figure in Kufa. During Muslim ibn Aqeel's stay in Kufa, he was the one who delivered the last letter of the Kufans to Imam Hussain (AS) in Mecca and brought back the Imam's reply to Muslim. After delivering the letters, he decided to join the Imam's caravan and sacrifice his life for him.

On the night of Ashura, when Imam Hussain (AS) said that anyone who wishes to leave is free to do so, Saeed ibn Abdullah stood up and spoke historic and passionate words: 'By God, we will not leave you until God knows we have preserved the sanctity of His Prophet regarding you. By God, if I knew I would be killed, resurrected, burned, and my ashes scattered to the wind, and this was repeated seventy times, I would never separate from you'.

Saeed's peak of devotion was manifested on the day of Ashura during the noon prayer. When Abu Thumama al-Sa'idi reminded the Imam of the prayer time, the Imam said: 'You remembered the prayer, may God make you among the praying ones'. Then the Imam and his companions stood for prayer, while the enemy army cowardly targeted them with arrows.

According to the report in Al-Irshad, Saeed ibn Abdullah, along with Zuhayr ibn Qayn, stood in front of the Imam to act as human shields. Seeing every arrow coming towards the Imam, Saeed threw himself in front of it. He took so many arrows to his chest, head, and arms that after the prayer ended, he fell to the ground with thirteen arrows in his body.

While he was dying, Imam Hussain (AS) came to his side. With his last breaths, Saeed asked: 'O son of the Prophet, have I fulfilled my pledge?' The Imam, with tearful eyes, replied: 'Yes, you are in Paradise before me'. Hearing this glad tiding, Saeed smiled and attained martyrdom.
quote: (In his last moments to the Imam:) O son of the Messenger of God, have I fulfilled my pledge?
